Off Calendar Jan-07-2026 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar being set for plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk: Damon Carter; Dept. 505 - Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:27am and concluding at 9:43am.

Plaintiff Harford Fire Insurance Company appearing represented by Christopher J. Brennan, Esq. via zoom
Defendant Carlos McClatchy not appearing.

Counsel for Plaintiff presents summary of case and an offer of proof.

Court examines witness for Plaintiff Insurance Adjuster PHATSALY PHAHTHIP who appeared via zoom and confirms statements made by counsel.

The matter is submitted.

Based on the documents filed, the statement made by Plaintiff and its Counsel, consideration and good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Harford Fire Insurance Company, and against Defendant Carlos McClatchy, as follows:

Damages: $5,753,120.29
Prejudgment Interest: $10
Attorney Fees: $0
Costs: $9,801.33

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $5,753,120.29

Proposed judgment signed and filed this date.

MOTION TO STRIKE GENERAL DENIAL
Off Calendar Jul-15-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFFS HARTFORD FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUBROGEE FOR ITS INSURED, AND PIETRO MANAGEMENT COMPANY's MOTION TO STRIKE GENERAL DENIAL.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Hartford Fire Insurance Company's unopposed motion to strike defendant Carlos McClatchy's general denial is granted. The court previously issued a discovery order and sanctions against McClatchy, who has not yet responded to the outstanding discovery. It appears to the court that lesser sanctions will not achieve McClatchy's compliance with his discovery obligations. The answer is accordingly stricken.

Order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiffs Form Rogs (Set One), Special Rogs (Set One) And Request For Production Of Documents (Set One); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Request For Sanctions Against Defendant
Off Calendar Mar-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF HARTFORD FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY , INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUBROGEE FOR ITS INSURED, PIETRO MANAGEMENT COMPANY's Motion To Compel Responses To Plaintiff's Form Rogs (Set One), Special Rogs (Set One) And Request For Production Of Documents (Set One); Request For Sanctions Against Defendant.

Plaintiff Hartford Fire Insurance Company's motion to compel defendant Carlos McClatchy to respond to its Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One; and Requests for Production of Documents, Set One, is granted. Defendant failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2030.290(a) & 2031.300(a).) Defendant is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, accompanied by documents responsive to the requests for production within 15 court days of entry of this order.

Plaintiff also seeks sanctions. CCP 2030.290(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to interrogatories . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." CCP 2031.300(c) contains an analogous provision with respect to requests for production. The court does not find substantial justification here. The court reduces the sanctions amount because no opposition or reply briefs were filed. The court awards sanctions of $1480.75 to Plaintiff payable by Defendant. Defendant shall remit sanctions within 15 court days of entry of this order.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Establishing Admissions
Off Calendar Mar-12-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF HARTFORD FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY , INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUBROGEE FOR ITS INSURED, PIETRO MANAGEMENT COMPANY Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Establishing Admissions

PLAINTIFF HARTFORD FIRE INSURANCE COMPANY, INDIVIDUALLY AND AS SUBROGEE FOR ITS INSURED, PIETRO MANAGEMENT COMPANY's Motion For Order Establishing Admissions.

Plaintiff Hartford Insurance Company's motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in its Requests for Admissions, Set One, is granted. Defendant Carlos McClatchy failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (CCP 2033.280(a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs, Set One, is deemed admitted by Plaintiff. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).)

Plaintiff also seeks sanctions. CCP 2033.280(c) further provides: "It is mandatory that the court impose a monetary sanction under Chapter 7 (commencing with Section 2023.010) on the party or attorney, or both, whose failure to serve a timely response to requests for admission necessitated this motion." The court awards sanctions but reduces the requested amount. Plaintiff's estimate was based on the assumption that the motion would be opposed, and it was not. The court awards $1480.75 in sanctions payable by Defendant to Plaintiff. Defendant shall remit sanctions within 15 court days of entry of this order.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
